rationale: Both candidates independently flag the audio_vae.py:141 config-mutation issue that reviewer amy-why-3459 raised, but only X also flags the dropped-language-field concern in serving_speech.py:2655 (asking if intentional, requesting a test/doc, mirroring the actual GT reviewer comment) — Y instead frames that removal as already 'clean' and consistent, completely missing a concern a human reviewer explicitly raised and the author had to justify. Y is more accurately calibrated on the config-mutation point (correctly notes it's functionally harmless, matching the maintainer's actual resolution) versus X's overstated 'major/fix required' framing, but this precision edge doesn't offset X's clearly better recall of the GT's core concerns. Both are grounded and actionable with file:line specificity.
